canonical_definition: The tendency of LLMs to rely on parametric knowledge rather than faithfully reasoning over provided contextual information, especially when the two conflict.
papers: Improving Model Evaluation usingSMARTFiltering of Benchmark Datasets
VoCoT: Unleashing Visually Grounded Multi-Step Reasoning in Large Multi-Modal Models

definitions.text: The latent tendency of LLMs to rely on pre-trained internal knowledge even when it conflicts with reliable external evidence, potentially undermining retrieval-augmented generation.
